deleteConfirm.title: Confirm Deletion
deleteConfirm.message: Are you sure you want to delete this item? This action cannot be undone.

helpSubtitles.addZone: Creates a visual zone for grouping nodes
helpSubtitles.generateAi: Imports text and builds the graph automatically
helpSubtitles.play: Tests the story in preview mode
helpSubtitles.theme: Switches between light and dark mode

helpDescription.addScene: Use this button to add a new choice passage to your graph. Each scene can have several options to advance the story.
helpDescription.addScript: Use this button to add a JavaScript node. It is useful for advanced logic and variable manipulation inside the story.
helpDescription.addStyle: This button creates a node for adding custom styles to your game and controlling the appearance of graph elements.
helpDescription.addZone: Use this button to add a new visual zone to group your story scenes together. Child nodes within a zone will move together with the zone.
helpDescription.generateAi: Use this button to open the AI import. It generates a graph from story text using a language model.
helpDescription.play: This button opens play mode. You can navigate the narrative as a player and test available choices.
helpDescription.theme: Use this button to change the interface theme. Dark mode is easier on the eyes at night, light mode works well during the day.
helpDescription.settings: Opens the settings panel. Here you can toggle secret nodes, view flow errors, and control editor options.

helpSystem.intro: These nodes control the invisible engine and structure of your game:
helpSystem.storyInit: The engine starts here. This is the required place to declare all story variables before the first screen.
helpSystem.storyData: Stores important technical metadata in JSON format (such as the format version and start passage).
helpSystem.storyCaption: What you write here stays visible in the game sidebar. Great for showing inventory, sanity, or stats in real time.

dataPanel.title: Data Engine
dataPanel.importHelpLine1: You can import your Twine code (Twee format) in two ways:
dataPanel.importHelpBullet1: Paste the text directly into the box.
dataPanel.importHelpBullet2: Drag and drop a .twee file into the area.
dataPanel.importHelpWarning: Warning: Importing will overwrite the current graph.
dataPanel.placeholder: Paste or drop a .twee file here

validationHelp.title: Logical Validator
validationHelp.subtitle: Graph math engine
validationHelp.line1: This algorithm walks every possible path of your story while simulating variable state (the player inventory).
validationHelp.line2: It warns you if it detects dead ends or permanently blocked routes to avoid launching an impossible-to-complete story.

dataPanel.removeLanguageHint: Click to completely delete this locale column

languages.title: Locale Manager
languages.subtitle: Adding custom targets
languages.line1: Type any locale identifier (like CZ, FR, JP) and click "+" to append a dynamic translation column across your database.
languages.warning: Warning: Clicking an active language badge will purge its column and all associated data permanently!

table.title: Localization Engine
table.subtitle: Dynamic placeholders mapping
table.line1: Define abstract keys to bypass raw hardcoded text lines. Inside your passage choice layouts, parse them using:

dataPanelPrompts.newKeyName: Enter the new translation key (e.g., scene_intro):

settingsModal.title: Settings

secretNodes.title: Secret Nodes
secretNodes.subtitle: Toggle system node visibility
secretNodes.text: Switch the visibility of special Twine system nodes like StoryInit or StoryData. Turning this off declutters the screen while you write the story.

flowAlerts.title: Flow Alerts
flowAlerts.subtitle: Logical error warnings
flowAlerts.text: Controls the red box in the data panel. When active, the system warns you about permanently blocked options or unreachable narrative sections.

visualLogic.title: Visual Logic Editor
visualLogic.subtitle: Simplified editing of conditions and variables
visualLogic.text: Enables a tab in the Inspector that allows you to configure narratives, variables, and choice conditions visually, without having to write SugarCube code directly.

visualBlocksMode.title: Logic Blocks Mode
visualBlocksMode.subtitle: Scratch-style block editor
visualBlocksMode.text: Transforms the visual editor into a structure of blocks (narrative, variables, conditions, and choices) resembling puzzle-like components.

adjacencyList.title: Adjacency List
adjacencyList.subtitle: Graph math structure
adjacencyList.text: Opens a technical data panel showing how nodes are connected behind the scenes. Useful for debugging route algorithms.

devSimulation.title: Dev Simulation
devSimulation.subtitle: Browser console testing
devSimulation.text: Enables a button that runs invisible simulations of all possible paths. Results are printed to the browser console (F12).

dangerZone.title: Delete Project
dangerZone.subtitle: Warning: irreversible action
dangerZone.text: This button permanently destroys all unexported app data, clears local browser storage, and reloads the page.

settingsModal.dangerZoneText: This button permanently destroys all unexported app data, clears local browser storage, and reloads the page.

aiImportModal.title: AI: Text to Graph
aiImportModal.storyPlaceholder: Once upon a time, an adventurer arrived at a fork in the road. If you go left...
aiImportModal.helpSubtitle: How to write for the best results
aiImportModal.helpLine1: Write your story in a continuous narrative, but make branches clear.
aiImportModal.helpExampleText: "You wake in a cell. You can try to break the door or shout for a guard. If you break the door, you find a corridor. If you shout, the guard chains you."
aiImportModal.helpLine2: The AI will convert your descriptive logic into a structured graph of nodes.

errors.noModel: Please enter the Ollama model name.

playMode.noNarrative: No narrative text defined in this passage.
playMode.devHelpLine1: Normal mode shows only accessible options. In Dev mode, all options are visible to help test flow.
playMode.devHelpLine2: Use Ctrl + Shift + D to toggle Dev Mode quickly.

inspector.title: Cell Inspector
inspector.label: Node Name (Label)
inspector.localVarHint: Create a variable only in this node
inspector.selectNode: Select a node on the graph to inspect or edit its properties.

deleteConfirm.nodeMessage: Are you sure you want to delete this story scene node? All graph edges connected to it will be purged synchronously.

label.title: Node Name
label.subtitle: Unique identifier
label.text: The node label serves as the reference destination for Twine links and navigation. Avoid duplicate names.

type.title: Node Type
type.subtitle: Block Function
type.text: Defines whether this block contains narrative story or technical code.

tags.title: Tags
tags.subtitle: Metadata
tags.text: Used for special behaviors. Ex: start, secreto.

content.title: Content
content.subtitle: Where the magic happens
content.text: Write your story, choices, or code here.

help.title: Content Syntax Guide
help.subtitle: Supported formats inside story nodes
help.variablesText: You can declare and print dynamic data in real-time within the simulator memory:
help.variablesExample3: Direct text injection: "You have $gold coins in your pocket."
help.linksText: To create connections between scenes and generate automatic navigation buttons:
help.linksExample1: Standard Twine link: [[Go to the Market]]
help.linksExample2: Link with custom display text: [[Talk to Merchant|Market]] or [[Talk to Merchant -> Market]]
help.i18nText: To ensure the story adapts to active simulator locale swaps seamlessly, inject the localization macro into narratives or buttons:
help.logicText: Control player choices visibility and access by validating system states:
help.logicExample1: Standard conditional block: <<if $gold >= 10>>Appears if condition is met<</if>>

prompts.varError:
Error: The variable does not exist in StoryInit!

Create it there first or enable the "Create a variable only in this node" checkbox.

alerts.resetConfirm: Warning: This will delete all current progress. Continue?
alerts.aiInvalid: AI returned invalid output. Please try again.
alerts.noModel: Please enter an Ollama model to use.
alerts.specialNodeExists: The special node already exists in the project.
alerts.validationSuccess:
Story is consistent! All paths are reachable.
✓ {count} end(s) detected: {ends}
alerts.validationNoEnd: No flow issues found, but no ending was detected. The story may be stuck in a loop.
alerts.playModeBlocked:
ACCESS BLOCKED:

Player Mode cannot start because some nodes have syntax errors (highlighted in orange).

Check the Inspector and fix invalid links before testing the story.

translationMatrix.title: Localization Matrix
translationMatrix.noReference: No reference text found in source locale.
translationMatrix.emptySelectionHint: Select any table cell to inspect or edit its content without popups.

deleteConfirm.keyMessage: Are you sure you want to completely erase this localization key? This will permanently delete its translation contents across all language tables.

help.title: Matrix Workspace Help
help.subtitle: Multi-language management configuration
help.line1: Select any table cell to translate its content directly in the inspector panel.
help.line2: The first configured column functions as the source language of reference for the simulation.

exportModal.title: Export Twee Story
exportModal.description: Select the compilation format for the final Twine file (.twee):

templatePrompt.title: Base Template Prompt
templatePrompt.question: Do you want to see a base template?
templatePrompt.instructionsLine1: To add or configure a base template, you can edit the initialNodes array in:
templatePrompt.instructionsLine2: This array defines the initial structure of nodes loaded when the local storage workspace is empty (or reset). You can customize it with your own default passages, text templates, or styling settings.

variablesModal.title: Story Variables
variablesModal.renameHint: Renaming propagates automatically across the entire project.
variablesModal.noVarsGlobalDesc: Create global variables using the "+ Create variable" button above.
variablesModal.noVarsLocalDesc: Activate global variables in the current scene to modify them locally.

visualBlocks.narrativePlaceholder: Write your story scene text here...
visualBlocks.noChoices: No choices. Connect this node to others or click add below.

tutorial.promptQuestion: Would you like to take a guided tutorial to learn how to use Plot-in-a-pot?
tutorial.dashboardSubtitle: Learn the core features of the interactive story editor step-by-step.
tutorial.completeText: Excellent job! You have completed this tutorial. Feel free to try another or start building your own story.
tutorial.t1Desc: Learn how to select passages and modify titles and descriptions.
tutorial.t1Step1Desc: Double-click or single-click the 'Start' node in the canvas to open the Inspector panel on the right.
tutorial.t1Step2Desc: In the Inspector panel, locate the 'Etiqueta' (Label) input field. Delete the text and type 'Entrada' as the new title of this node.
tutorial.t1Step3Desc: Great! Now edit the main text content inside the narrative editor text area. Type: 'Estás no início da aventura.' to set the passage text.
tutorial.t1Step4Desc: Fantastic! You successfully updated your first story node title and description. Click Finish to complete this module.
tutorial.t2Desc: Learn to connect scenes and find logical issues.
tutorial.t2Step1Desc: Connect two scenes visually. Click and drag a line from the circular handle at the bottom of the 'Start' node to the top handle of the 'Corredor' node, then click Confirm in the modal.
tutorial.t2Step2Desc: Let's expand the story map! Click the '+ Adicionar Cena' (+ Add Scene) button in the TopBar to spawn a new scene node on the board.
tutorial.t2Step3Desc: Excellent. Now click and drag from the circular handle at the bottom of the 'Corredor' node to the top of your newly added scene, and click Confirm in the modal.
tutorial.t2Step4Desc: Now let's check for unreachable nodes or loops. Click the 'Validar Lógica' (Validate Logic) button on the left panel (Data Engine).
tutorial.t2Step5Desc: Great job! The validator confirmed your story is logically consistent and all passages are reachable. Click Finish to complete.
tutorial.t3Desc: Learn about special system nodes (StoryInit, StoryTitle, StoryData) and visually declare variables using buttons.
tutorial.t3Step1Desc: System nodes are special nodes that configure your story. Click and select the 'StoryTitle' node. In the content editor, you can change the title of your game.
tutorial.t3Step2Desc: Now, click and select the 'StoryData' node. This node contains metadata like the Twine story format (SugarCube) and starting node config in JSON format.
tutorial.t3Step3Desc: Excellent! Now click and select the 'StoryInit' node. This node is where we initialize global variables (like gold, health, inventory) when the story starts.
tutorial.t3Step4Desc: Instead of writing code manually, you can use visual buttons! With the 'StoryInit' node selected, click the 'Criar Variável' (Create Variable) button at the top of the content text area in the Inspector on the right.
tutorial.t3Step5Desc: In the Variables modal, write 'moedas' as the variable name, set its value to '50', and click Confirm. This will automatically generate the code for you inside the StoryInit node!
tutorial.t3Step6Desc: Great job! You created the variable $moedas. Notice how the code '<<set $moedas to 50>>' was automatically generated in StoryInit. The 'Start' node will now display this variable. You have completed this system nodes guide!
tutorial.t4Desc: Test the game interactively and localized in multiple languages.
tutorial.t4Step1Desc: Let's see your story in action! Click the 'Play' button in the top bar to open the interactive preview simulator.
tutorial.t4Step2Desc: This project supports multiple languages. In the Play Mode panel on the right, change the 'Story Language' dropdown from English (EN) to Portuguese (PT). Notice how the choice button text translates dynamically from 'Enter Room' to 'Entrar na Sala'!
tutorial.t4Step3Desc: Now, let's navigate to the next scene. Click the translated choice button 'Entrar na Sala' in the simulator.
tutorial.t4Step4Desc: Excellent! You entered the 'Room' scene. Now, close the play mode simulator by clicking the 'End Test' button in the right panel.
tutorial.t5Desc: Learn to visually drag conditional choices based on player inventory variables.
tutorial.t5Step1Desc: Select the 'Portão' node by single-clicking or double-clicking it on the graph.
tutorial.t5Step2Desc: Click and drag from the circular handle at the bottom of the 'Portão' node to the top of the 'Sucesso' node. In the connection modal, select 'Condicional ⚡', select variable 'temChave', operator 'is', value 'true', type 'Abrir Portão' as choice text, and click 'Confirmar Ligação'.
tutorial.t5Step3Desc: Great job! The SugarCube conditional code was visually generated. Now let's test it! Click 'Play' in the top bar to enter play mode, advance to 'Portão', and see the gate unlock since you found the key.
